issues.title: [LFX Project] Enhancing the Knative func CLI Experience
issues.body:
## Description:

[Knative Functions](https://github.com/knative/func) enables you to easily create, build, and deploy stateless, event-driven functions as Knative Services by using the [`func` CLI](https://knative.dev/docs/functions/install-func/).
With this LFX project, we aim to conduct a comprehensive UX evaluation of the func CLI. The focus will be on identifying usability issues, understanding developer workflows, and gathering structured feedback on CLI interactions. The findings will help improve the CLI's intuitiveness, efficiency, and user satisfaction.

## Expected Outcome: 

The primary deliverable will be a detailed report documenting the `func` CLI’s usability findings. This report should include, but is not limited to:
- Assessment of CLI command organization, structure, and discoverability for new and experienced users
- Review of help text quality and completeness across all commands
- Mapping of common developer workflows with identified pain points
- Evaluation of error handling and the user guidance during failures
- Comparison with industry best practices for CLI design, with recommendations for closing identified gaps

## Recommended Skills: 

Experience with command-line interfaces, familiarity with UX evaluation/research methodologies, knowledge of cloud-native and Knative concepts, strong analytical, technical writing and documentation skills.

issues.title: [bug] The Knative bi-weekly UX WG meeting reminder slack bot is sending reminders every week

labels.name: kind/bug
labels.description: Categorizes issue or PR as related to a bug.

issues.body:
<!-- 
Are you using Knative? If you do, we would love to know!
https://github.com/knative/community/issues/new?template=ADOPTERS.yaml&title=%5BADOPTERS%5D%3A+%24%7BCOMPANY+NAME+HERE%7D
-->
As described in the title, the Knative bi-weekly UX WG meeting reminder slack bot is sending reminders every week.

The yaml file that is used to configure this github action is [here](https://github.com/knative/ux/blob/main/.github/workflows/bi-weekly-UX-WG-meeting-reminder.yaml) 

The reminder is sent to the CNCF slack #knative-ux channel. One example message is https://cloud-native.slack.com/archives/C05MW1AT1T8/p1722431220346959.

The next/most recent bi-weekly UX WG meeting will be happening August 8th, 2024.

Warn: In order to test the feature, the PR has to be merged for GitHub action to run.

/good-first-issue
/help-wanted
/kind bug

issues.title: Knative Community Handbook
issues.body:
Hello everyone!

As discussed in our last WG meeting, we will be developing a Knative Community Handbook. This handbook will serve as a great introductory resource for anyone looking to get involved in the Knative Community. 

Our main sources of information and inspiration for this handbook are:
- [Knative Community Documentation](https://knative.dev/docs/community/)
- [Layers5 Community Handbook](https://layer5.io/community/handbook)

I have drafted the following sections that could be included in our handbook. Please share your opinions on these and suggest any additional sections that you think should be included:

1. How to Get Involved
2. About Knative
3. Where to Ask Questions
4. Meet Kuack, the Knative Duck
5. How to contribute
6. Meeting Schedule
7. Community Roles
8. FAQs
9. Project Overview
10. Mentorship Programs

Note: These sections are not sorted by priority... they are just a consolidation of some ideas.

Looking forward to your feedback!

issues.title: Finalizing Improvement for Onboarding Materials
issues.body:
This issue focuses on finalizing the improved onboarding materials for Knative Eventing. The goal is to ensure that all materials are of high quality and ready for widespread use.  It may be necessary to make changes to specific tasks, including adding new tasks or removing existing ones.



### Tasks:

**1. Final Review:**

Conduct a final review of all onboarding materials.
Ensure accuracy, clarity, and completeness.

**2. Polish and Format:**

Make any last-minute adjustments to the materials.
Ensure consistency in style, format, and presentation.

### Link to Main Issue:
[Onboarding Experience Study for Knative Eventing #130](https://github.com/orgs/knative/projects/82/views/1?visibleFields=%5B%22Title%22%2C%22Assignees%22%2C%22Status%22%2C%22Linked+pull+requests%22%2C%22Repository%22%2C118964326%2C118961005%5D&pane=issue&itemId=70924659#130)

issues.title: Sample App: Adding the graphics to the sample app tutorial
issues.body:
In order to make the the Sample App tutorial more engaging and visually appealing for the readers, and also to celebrate the recent publishing our Knative's new friend: Kuack, I am proposing hope incorporate Kuack into the design, and let him become the small "teacher duck", as I am sure he will add so much fun!

We can create a series of images featuring Kuack in various facial expressions and postures. These images will not only enrich our documentation but also have the potential to be transformed into stickers.

Here are some sample designs I have:


![Image](https://github.com/knative/ux/assets/36619969/fe50ec8b-be11-49b2-bb07-c9a26b06534a)

---

![Image](https://github.com/knative/ux/assets/36619969/bd38e336-c780-40b4-86d5-113e6a74fd35)

issueComments.body:
I’d like to help with the finalization of the onboarding materials from a first-time user / product UX perspective.

I can focus on:

clarity and completeness for newcomers

consistency of terminology and structure

overall onboarding flow (problem → overview → first success → next steps)

I’ll review the materials as if I were onboarding to Knative Eventing for the first time and propose concrete improvements (reordering, wording, missing context, and small UX additions like “expected outcomes”).

Does that sound useful? If so, I can either leave structured feedback on this issue or open a focused PR with doc improvements.
